content:
✨ macshot | Swift
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enhancing the screenshot and screen recording workflow on macOS with advanced editing, annotation, and sharing features.
✨ Key Features: • Annotation tools • Screen recording with video editor • Scroll capture • Upload to cloud services (Google Drive, S3-compatible) • OCR + translation
📖 Summary: Macshot is a feature-rich, native macOS application for taking screenshots and recording the screen. It offers extensive annotation tools, screen recording with a built-in editor, OCR capabilities, automatic PII redaction, scroll capture, and cloud uploading, all without subscriptions and built with Swift and AppKit for a lightweight, native experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 sistine | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling touchscreen functionality on a MacBook without dedicated hardware.
✨ Key Features: • Turns MacBook into a touchscreen • Uses $1 of hardware • Utilizes computer vision • Leverages built-in webcam • Translates video feed into touch events
📖 Summary: Project Sistine transforms a MacBook into a functional touchscreen using minimal hardware and computer vision. It leverages the MacBook's built-in webcam and a small mirror to detect finger proximity and touches on the screen, translating them into input events.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 RadioPlayerV3 | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Streaming music and radio content into Telegram group voice chats for continuous playback.
✨ Key Features: • Playlist with queuing and 24/7 radio support • YouTube Live stream support • Auto-fallback to radio when playlist ends • Persistent playback • Interactive controls
📖 Summary: RadioPlayerV3 is an advanced Telegram bot designed to stream music and radio directly into group voice chats. It supports playlists, YouTube live streams, and ensures persistent playback, making it ideal for continuous audio streaming in Telegram communities.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
